column8: Here's a problem for you. Imagine you're in a prison cell with two doors. There's a duard at each door. You can ask one of the guards onr question, and then you can go through one door. ____________you choose the right door, you can go free.
column9: _____________, if you choose the wrong door, you have to stay in prison forever!
column10: And it gets worse! ___________one of the guards always tells the truth, the other one always lies. And you don't know which one one tells the truth.
column11: It's an impossible situation - isn't it? No, it's not. But________________you're extremely careful, you might choose the wrong door.
column12: ______________you're absolutely sure it's the door to freedom.
column13: And you'll only know it's the right door____________you ask the right question.
column14: ________________or not knowing if the guard always tells the truth or always lies, you'll always get an answer that will help you.
column15: ____________you get the answer, go through the other door. You'll be free!
